stories.title: Trump’s call to reduce US troops in Germany shocks Pentagon
stories.summary: President Donald Trump’s announcement Wednesday that he was considering pulling some U.S. troops out of Germany stunned defense officials, who scrambled to figure out if the president was serious about following through on his threats this time. Trump’s social media post was the first that many had heard of a potential new push to take hundreds, if not thousands, of American troops out of Germany, according to three defense officials. It strongly contrasts with a recently concluded monthslong re

stories.title: Anger and apathy on UK campaign trail force candidates to keep it local
stories.summary: SURREY, England — There are impassioned election fights going on in this leafy part of England — but you’d be hard-pressed to find a voter jumping for joy at the prospect. ​Across Surrey’s county and borough councils there’s a united theme: apathy and anger at politicians of all stripes. Rising costs, struggling public services, and general disillusionment with national politics mean even in well-to-do Surrey suburbs — once Conservative strongholds —  the public appears checked o

stories.title: Venice Biennale jury quits over decision to allow Russia’s participation
stories.summary: The entire international jury of the Venice Biennale resigned on Thursday in the latest escalation of a months-long political fight over Russia’s return to the exhibition. The exodus marks one of the most dramatic moves yet in a dispute that has pitted members of the jury, the Biennale Foundation and the Italian government against one another. At the heart of the conflict is a controversial decision to reopen the Russian pavilion, which has been closed since Moscow launched its invasion of Ukrai

stories.title: ‘Don’t fall!’: foil boarders describe hair-raising shark chase caught on video off California coast
stories.summary: Foil boarders were pursued by shark – likely a great white – off Santa Barbara before it lost interest and swam away Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox Ron Takeda and Tavis Boise were a few miles off the coast of Santa Barbara when they noticed the large mass trailing behind them. “Tavis, is it a dolphin?” asked Takeda as he stood on his foil board, a specialized form of surfing, propelling himself through the waves. Boise, who was filming their run, re

stories.title: Fema employees who criticized Trump cuts reinstated after months on leave
stories.summary: Workers wrote ‘Katrina declaration’, warning that funding cuts made US dangerously unprepared for natural disasters Fourteen employees with the US Federal Emergency Management Agency returned to work this week, after spending eight months on administrative leave for signing a public letter criticising the Trump administration . The so-called “Katrina declaration”, sent last August to members of Congress and a federal council formed to help determine Fema’s future, was written as a rebuke from th

stories.title: Trump launches fresh attack on Merz after threatening US troop reduction in Germany – as it happened
stories.summary: US president uses social media post to criticise chancellor over Ukraine, immigration and ‘interfering’ in Iran conflict The Commission was also asked about yesterday’s meeting of Hungary’s incoming prime minister, Péter Magyar. But we didn’t get much more than what we saw in yesterday’s social media posts from Magyar and the EU’s Ursula von der Leyen. Continue reading...

stories.title: The missing Ukrainian reporter, the Russian prison – and a vital lesson learned about journalism in a dangerous age | Laurent Richard
stories.summary: To do our job, investigative journalists are learning to work across borders. This isn’t just about the truth, it’s about staying alive Laurent Richard is a journalist and the director of the Forbidden Stories consortium In February 2025, the body of journalist Viktoriia Roshchyna was finally returned to Ukraine after months of uncertainty. She was one of 757 Ukrainian casualties handed over by Russian authorities as part of an exchange of prisoners and the dead. Roshchyna had disappeared in the

stories.title: RSF: Hungary third lowest-ranked in EU in terms of press freedom
stories.summary: At the same time, the annual report of Reporters Without Borders described the Tisza Party's election victory as a unique opportunity for the country.

stories.summary: „Talán kicsit megkopott, alulértékelt, és sokszor mi magunk sem voltunk méltók hozzá” – írja a távozó miniszter.

stories.title: Alapvetően az NKA egy nagyon jól kitalált rendszer, de minden rendszer annyit ér, ahogy működtetik
stories.summary: A Telex After legújabb adásában a Cseh Tamás Program korábbi vezetője és a legnagyobb magyar független lemezkiadó ügyvezetője reagálnak az NKA-botrányra, amelynek részeként Hankó Balázs és Bús Balázs tudtával milliárdokat szórtak ki a Fidesz kulturális és politikai holdudvarának úgy, hogy megpróbálták az egészet titokban tartani.

stories.title: Zelenszkij jelezte, valamilyen formában találkozni fog Magyar Péterrel
stories.summary: Az ukrán elnök azt mondta, minden ukrajnai magyar kisebbséget érintő kérdéssel foglalkoznak, de szerinte nincs valódi probléma.

stories.title: Magyar Péter egyetemi évfolyamtársa, sógora, ügyvédje, a Tisza jogi vezetője – bemutatjuk az új igazságügyi minisztert
stories.summary: Melléthei-Barna Márton komoly nemzetközi ügyvédi irodákban dolgozott, majd rögtön 2024-ben csatlakozott a Tisza Párthoz. Az előválasztáson kikapott, de listáról bejutott a parlamentbe. Most az ő feladata lesz a jogállam és a jogbiztonság helyreállítása.

stories.title: Összeállt a teljes csapat, ők lesznek Magyar Péter kormányának miniszterei
stories.summary: Összesen 16-an vannak. Az Orbán-kormányokhoz képest újdonság, hogy újra külön tárcát kap az egészségügy, az oktatás és a környezetvédelem is. Magyar Péter helyettese is a tárcavezetők közül került ki.

stories.title: Leszerelt a honvédségtől az egyik legmagasabb szintre jutó magyar katona
stories.summary: Porkoláb Imre korábban a Pentagonban is dolgozott, az elmúlt években pedig Orbán Viktor nemzetbiztonsági főtanácsadójának munkatársa volt.

stories.summary: Azt szeretnék, hogy az új kormány érdemben vonja be őket az igazságszolgáltatást érintő ügyekbe.

stories.summary: The EU, however, had hoped that the new Hungarian government would lend its support to the launch of Ukraine’s accession process.

stories.title: The Guardian view on the politics of central Europe: in search of a new left | Editorial
stories.summary: Social democratic parties are suffering an almost total wipeout, as rightwing nationalism flourishes on the EU’s eastern flank Péter Magyar’s historic defeat of Viktor Orbán in Hungary’s recent election was rightly celebrated in progressive circles and beyond. For the global far right, which has been steadily gaining power and influence for over a decade, this was a significant reverse. But it was no victory for the left. A former member of Mr Orbán’s Fidesz party, Mr Magyar will lead a cen

stories.title: Avalanche deaths have surged in the Alps this season. Here’s why | Johan Gaume
stories.summary: As an ex-pro snowboarder, I know the temptations of fresh snowfall. And as an avalanche researcher, I know how easily people can get into trouble Many avalanche accidents occur just after a storm, when blue-sky conditions return and people head to the backcountry to enjoy fresh powder. These kinds of accidents happen every year, despite warnings to those heading out into the snow. It is easy to dismiss some behaviour as reckless, and sometimes it is – but it is also human behaviour, and it is no

stories.summary: A Magyar Orvosi Kamara szerint szuverén, önrendelkezésre képes, a szakma értékeit képviselni, etikai tisztaságát őrizni tudó szakmai köztestület léte a betegek és az egész magyar társadalom érdeke.

stories.summary: Az EU azt szeretné, ha az új magyar kormány támogatná Ukrajna csatlakozási folyamatának megindítását.

stories.title: A szlovák példa alapján még nagyon sokat erősödhet a forint
stories.summary: Tizenöt éven keresztül gyengítették, ezért a lakosság rosszat gondol a forintról, pedig a kamatelőnye miatt páréves távlatban mindig a hazai deviza volt a jó választás. Most ráadásul tartós felértékelődés jöhet.

stories.title: Az Orbán-rezsimmel való mélypontom az átláthatósági törvény volt
stories.summary: Weiner Dánielt, a Balzac főszerkesztőjét és Doroszlai Emesét, a TASZ munkatársát kérdeztük arról, hogy a fiataloknak miért lett érdekes a politika, és miért élik meg rendszerváltásként április 12-ét.

stories.title: Hungary's Magyar meets EU's von der Leyen in high-stakes Brussels visit
stories.summary: Even before Peter Magyar takes office as Hungary's prime minister, he is looking to unlock €10 billion in EU funding ahead of an August deadline. Talks with Ursula von der Leyen come as part of a post-Orban EU reset.

stories.title: Germany’s Merz loses hard-won favor with Trump
stories.summary: BERLIN — German Chancellor Friedrich Merz has spent months walking a tightrope in his dealings with U.S. President Donald Trump. Now he appears to have fallen off. Merz has bent further than most European leaders to stay in Trump’s good graces, deeming it a strategic necessity to maintain close ties with an American leader known for holding grudges. But under rising political pressure at home the chancellor has also sharpened his criticism of Trump and of the war in Iran — both deeply unpop

stories.title: White House presses tech companies for support on AI-driven cyberattacks
stories.summary: The White House has asked a group of tech companies to answer a set of questions this week about how to ward off digital attacks that frontier AI tools could soon enable, according to four people with knowledge of discussions between the administration and the tech sector. The questions, from the White House’s Office of the National Cyber Director, focus on how specific sectors in the tech and cybersecurity industries can work with the White House to boost their defenses with AI, these people sa

stories.title: Brazil’s congress approves bill reducing prison sentence of former president Jair Bolsonaro
stories.summary: President Lula’s veto of the bill was overturned by Brazil’s congress and senate, meaning it now awaits confirmation by supreme court Brazil’s largely conservative congress has approved a bill reducing the prison sentence of the far-right former president Jair Bolsonaro, who was convicted last year of attempting a coup. The bill had initially been passed by congress in December, but President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva vetoed it in January in a symbolic move marking three years since Bolsonaro su

stories.title: New video shows accused gunman trying to storm White House press dinner as gunfire erupts – as it happened
stories.summary: This live blog is now closed. It came hours after the US supreme court decided that Louisiana’s creation of a second majority black congressional district to satisfy previously rulings relied too heavily on race and was “an unco

stories.title: Trump bets on quick Iran oil crunch. Experts see prolonged pain and rising costs.
stories.summary: The Trump administration insists that its naval blockade is putting the squeeze on Iran and that the regime is just a “matter of days” away from an energy crisis unless it capitulates. Energy experts are skeptical and say the White House is misreading both the timing of the harm to the Iranian oil industry as well as the regime’s tolerance for pain. The standoff comes as benchmark oil prices hit a four-year high Thursday while the average price at the pump  jumped to $4.30 a gallon, up 27 c

stories.title: US charges Sinaloa governor and other Mexican officials with drug trafficking offences
stories.summary: Indictment accuses high-level officials in Sinaloa of offences such as drug trafficking, weapons offences and kidnapping The US justice department has charged the governor of Sinaloa and nine other current and former Mexican officials for alleged ties to the Sinaloa cartel, accusing them of aiding in the massive importation of illicit narcotics into the United States. Some officials were members of Mexico’s progressive ruling party, Morena, posing a political conundrum for Mexican president Clau

stories.title: Trump tells Merz to ‘fix his broken country’ in new attack on German chancellor
stories.summary: US president’s latest outburst comes a day after he suggested a ‘possible reduction’ in US troops in Germany Why does the US have military bases in Germany – and will Trump follow through on his threat? Donald Trump has again lashed out at Germany’s chancellor, Friedrich Merz, saying he should focus on “fixing his broken country” and trying to end the Russia-Ukraine war – and spend less time “interfering” in Iran. “The Chancellor of Germany should spend more time on ending the war with Russia/Uk

stories.title: Unrest in Alice Springs after Jefferson Lewis arrested over death of Kumanjayi Little Baby
stories.summary: Dozens of people gather outside hospital where 47-year-old was being treated five days after the five-year-old girl disappeared • Warning: This article contains references to and images of Indigenous Australians who have died Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast An angry crowd has clashed with police outside a hospital in Alice Springs where a 47-year-old man arrested by police in connection with the death of five-yea

stories.title: Tony Blair’s thinktank urges Labour to scrap ‘unaffordable’ pension triple lock
stories.summary: State pension was ‘built for a different era’, says former PM’s organisation amid pressure on government finances Labour has been urged by Tony Blair’s thinktank to scrap the pensions triple lock amid mounting pressure on government finances. With the Iran war threatening to derail public spending plans, the Tony Blair Institute (TBI) said the “unaffordable” manifesto pledge to maintain the triple lock should be torn up as part of a wider overhaul of the state pension. Continue reading...

stories.title: Syrian commission prepares war crimes case against notorious Assad official
stories.summary: Fadi Saqr is accused of mass killings of civilians in Tadamon, Damascus, where people say he must face justice A Syrian rights commission is preparing a case accusing Fadi Saqr, a militia leader within the Assad regime , of involvement in crimes against humanity and war crimes, a senior Syrian official has told the Guardian. Saqr is a former commander of the National Defence Forces (NDF) militia and is widely accused of involvement in the mass killing and forcible disappearance of civilians in t

stories.title: Queensland rejects key Bondi report recommendation as Albanese’s gun buyback flounders
stories.summary: State’s police minister says buyback ‘doesn’t focus on keeping guns out of the hands of terrorists and criminals’, leaving NSW only clear supporter of plan Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Queensland has rejected key recommendations from the Bondi royal commission’s interim report, insisting plans for a national gun buyback will not keep weapons “out of the hands of terrorists and criminals”. The report, handed do

stories.title: How Serbia's government dominates the country's media
stories.summary: Since Serbia's ruling SNS party came to power, it has tightened its grip on the media. With an election expected soon, experts fear that authorities will try to eliminate the last pockets of independent reporting.

stories.title: US: Trump signs DHS funding bill, ending partial shutdown
stories.summary: The legislation ends a partial government shutdown and restores funding to key government agencies. But Republicans are unhappy that ICE and Border Patrol have been excluded.

stories.title: Myanmar ex-leader Aung San Suu Kyi moved to house arrest, military says
stories.summary: The Nobel Peace Prize laureate has been in detention since she was ousted in a military coup in 2021.

stories.summary: Axios reported that US Central Command has prepared a plan for a wave of "short and powerful" strikes on Iran.

stories.title: Belgium plans to nationalise nuclear power plants
stories.summary: Prime Minister Bart De Wever said the move would see "less dependence on fossil fuel imports and more control over our own supply".

stories.title: Trump signs bill to end record shutdown over immigration enforcement
stories.summary: The shutdown caused chaos in airports across the US as politicians feuded over funds for Trump's immigration crackdown.

stories.title: Smoke rises over Russia’s Tuapse after Ukrainian drone strikes hit oil refinery
stories.summary: Plumes of black smoke rise over the southern Russian town of Tuapse after multiple Ukrainian drone strikes on a major oil refinery in the coastal area.

stories.title: Nigel Farage: Germany’s AfD is a ‘mixed bag’
stories.summary: LONDON — Nigel Farage described the far-right Alternative for Germany (AfD) party as a “mixed bag,” as the British politician eyes gains in crunch U.K. elections next Thursday. Germany’s AfD has an “utterly respectable and decent” cultural stance — but its ties with dictators like Russian President Vladimir Putin are “very, very difficult,” the right-wing Reform UK leader told Axel Springer CEO Mathias Döpfner for his podcast series  MD Me

stories.title: France and Spain want space reserved for EU firms in satellite frequencies
stories.summary: NICOSIA — France and Spain have teamed up in a bid to reserve space for European companies in an upcoming spectrum auction for mobile satellite communications, effectively pushing out U.S. players. The move comes as Brussels and EU capitals are mulling restrictions on non-EU players in a vast array of technologies from cloud computing to software, and grappling with the bloc’s reliance on U.S. and China-made tools. In 2009 the European Commission issued licenses for the 2 GHz spectrum bands used

stories.title: Trump removes tariffs on Scottish whisky after King Charles visit
stories.summary: President Donald Trump on Thursday said he will remove tariffs on Scotch whisky, touting the move as a celebration of the connection between the U.S. and United Kingdom to cap off King Charles III and Queen Camilla’s four-day U.S. visit. “In Honor of the King and Queen of the United Kingdom, who have just left the White House, soon headed back to their wonderful Country, I will be removing the Tariffs and Restrictions on Whiskey having to do with Scotland’s ability to work with the Commonwealth

stories.title: Israel intercepts and detains crews of Gaza aid flotilla near Crete
stories.summary: Global Sumud Flotilla describes interception as ‘violent raid’ while Turkey condemns it as ‘act of piracy’ Israeli forces have intercepted and detained the crews of at least 22 boats near the Greek island of Crete from a flotilla that is attempting to break Israel’s maritime blockade of the Gaza Strip to deliver humanitarian aid. The Global Sumud Flotilla, consisting of about 58 vessels carrying people from across 70 countries, departed from Italy on Sunday. Continue reading...

stories.title: Four rescued from Seine after bus plunges into river near Paris
stories.summary: Vehicle was travelling through Juvisy-sur-Orge when it veered off the road into the river Four people have been rescued from the Seine near Paris after a bus driven by a trainee driver collided with a parked vehicle before plunging into the river. The bus was travelling through the town of Juvisy-sur-Orge, south-east of the French capital, on Thursday when it veered off the road into the Seine, prosecutors said. Continue reading...

stories.title: Georg Baselitz – a life in pictures
stories.summary: The German painter, who has died aged 88, captured the great rupture of the 20th century, which shaped a body of work that helped redefine postwar art Continue reading...

stories.title: ‘In every drop of paint he slurped, you see the Holocaust’: the genius and torments of Georg Baselitz
stories.summary: The German artist lived through Nazism and communism – and his horrific, shaming works, including a masturbating Hitler, forced his country to face its past. Yet in later life, he beautifully captured human frailty, portraying himself and his wife nude Georg Baselitz was a living thread of history and his death robs us of the truth he knew when we need it more than ever. He was one of the only two people I have spoken to for whom Nazi Germany was a living memory: Baselitz was born in 1938, makin

stories.title: Venice Biennale jury quits amid row over participation of Russia
stories.summary: Decision follows backlash from Italian government and European Commission The jury of the Venice Biennale has quit just days before the prestigious art exhibition is due to begin, amid a row over the decision to allow Russia to participate. The resignation of the five-member international jury was announced late on Thursday in a brief statement by the Venice Biennale organisers, and came a day after the Italian culture ministry sent inspectors to Venice in search of information about the decisio

stories.title: German artist Georg Baselitz dies aged 88
stories.summary: Prominent contemporary visual artist explored range of techniques across six decades of work Georg Baselitz obituary The German artist Georg Baselitz, whose expressive paintings and sculptures stirred controversy before winning him global acclaim and the admiration of politicians in high office, has died aged 88. The Thaddaeus Ropac gallery, which had a longstanding professional relationship with the artist, confirmed his death on Thursday. It said Baselitz had “defined German visual art for a g

stories.title: ‘A new chapter’: first commercial flight from US since 2019 lands in Venezuela
stories.summary: Four months after US capture of Nicolás Maduro, officials hail repairing of ties as airliner touches down in Caracas US and Venezuelan officials have hailed a new era in diplomatic relations as the first direct commercial flight between the two countries in more than seven years landed in Caracas. Nearly four months ago, US special forces attack helicopters and planes swept into the skies over Venezuela’s capital after Donald Trump ordered the capture of its president, Nicolás Maduro . Continue

stories.title: Martha’s rule may have saved more than 500 lives in England since 2024
stories.summary: Patient safety mechanism which gives patients the right to seek a second opinion having ‘lifesaving impact’, says health secretary ‘I am invoking Martha’s rule’: how a woman saved her father from near death in hospital More than 500 people have received potentially life-saving care thanks to Martha’s rule, which gives hospital patients the right to seek a second opinion about their health. They were moved to intensive care or a specialist unit after they, a loved one or a member of NHS staff tri

stories.title: MPs accuse South East Water leaders of incompetence over repeated outages
stories.summary: Parliamentary committee takes unusual step of declaring no confidence in executives at utility provider MPs have accused the leadership of South East Water of incompetence over repeated water outages for tens of thousands of customers, and expressed no confidence in their ability to reform the company. MPs from across the political spectrum said David Hinton, SEW’s chief executive, and the board of directors operated a culture of unaccountability at the company, which provides drinking water for

stories.title: Ukraine war briefing: Kyiv claims victory over ‘shadow grain fleet’ shipment to Israel
stories.summary: Panama-flagged Panormitis turns away without unloading as Kyiv vows to treat carriers of ‘stolen’ grain the same as Russian oil tankers. What we know on day 1,528 Continue reading...

stories.title: Scientology ‘speed running’ trend has LA abuzz and church unhappy
stories.summary: Religious group ‘reviewing all available remedies’ after clips of young people rushing its buildings in ‘raids’ go viral On any given day, Los Angeles’s Hollywood Boulevard teems with tourists and street performers clustered near the area’s many landmarks. But in recent months, the strip has been set abuzz for a new reason. Throngs of mostly adolescent boys and young men have been rushing the Church of Scientology’s international headquarters on the famed street. Continue reading...

stories.title: Australian hiker missing in Nova Scotia national park not heard from for two weeks
stories.summary: Denise Ann Williams, 62, was last heard from on 15 April, when she told her family she was travelling to the west coast of Cape Breton Island in Canada’s east A search is underway in Canada for a 62-year-old Australian woman who was reported missing on Tuesday while hiking in a coastal national park in the country’s south-east. Denise Ann Williams was last heard from on 15 April, when she told family she was travelling to Chéticamp, a fishing village on the west coast of Cape Breton Island in th

stories.title: Trump administration says hostilities in Iran ‘terminated’ ahead of war powers deadline
stories.summary: US president faced a 60-day deadline on Friday to end the Iran war or make the case to Congress for extending it A US-Iran ceasefire that began in early April has “terminated” hostilities between the two sides for the purposes of an approaching congressional war powers deadline, a senior official of the Trump administration said on Thursday. Donald Trump faced a deadline on Friday to end the Iran war or make the case to Congress for extending it, but the date was most likely to pass without alte

stories.title: Australia news live: Jefferson Lewis was unconscious when apprehended, police say; Kumanjayi Little Baby’s grandfather calls for calm in Alice Springs

Police said they were told a driver of a white ute allegedly filled large drums and jerry cans with 915 litres of diesel, valued at over $2,870 (or about $3.14 a litre), without paying at about 7.15pm on 11 April. stories.title: Canada to create powerful financial crimes agency as US weakens its approach
stories.summary: Cryptocurrency ATMs also face ban, after public inquiry found Canada lacked anti-money-laundering strategy Canada is to establish a new and powerful law enforcement agency to investigate financial crime, in stark contrast to the US, where weakened federal investigators have struggled to pursue fraudsters and the White House has pardoned convicted money launderers . A bill to create the Financial Crimes Agency (FCA) completed its first reading in parliament this week. The legislation was introduc

stories.title: Oil price tops $126 a barrel after Trump warns Iran blockade could last ‘months’
stories.summary: Markets spooked as US president appears willing to keep up naval blockade and Iran keeps strait of Hormuz all but shut Business live – latest updates The global oil price hit $126 a barrel on Thursday, its highest level since 2022, after Donald Trump said the US blockade of Iranian ports could last for months and peace talks remained stalled. After surging more than 13% in 24 hours, the price of Brent crude futures reached its highest price since the war began on 28 February. Not since Russia’s

stories.title: Australians will pay more if Albanese prioritises fossil fuel projects, former oil and gas leaders warn
stories.summary: Exclusive: Government should accelerate clean energy to protect consumers from ongoing price shocks, they say Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Former oil and gas industry leaders, including senior executives from BP and Shell, are warning the Albanese government that Australians risk ongoing price shocks and higher costs if it prioritises fossil fuel development in response to the global energy crisis. stories.title: UK raises terror threat level following antisemitic attack
stories.summary: The Joint Terrorism Analysis Center raised the terror threat level to severe, which indicates that there's a high likelihood of an attack in the next six months.

stories.title: Saudi Arabia's public fund to withdraw LIV Golf funding
stories.summary: Public Investment Fund cites "current macro dynamics" as it slashes funding for breakaway golf tour. LIV isn't the only casualty of changing Saudi "investment priorities" amid economic turmoil and war in the Middle East.

stories.title: Kosovo to hold snap elections in June
stories.summary: Kosovo will soon hold its third parliamentary election in a year and a half. Prime Minister Albin Kurti was able to form a government in February, but now there's a political deadlock over who shoud be elected president.

stories.title: US prosecutors release new footage of Trump shooting suspect
stories.summary: The Justice Department has released unseen footage in an attempt to show that the secret service agent injured in the attack was not shot by friendly fire.

stories.summary: Christopher Okello Onyum had pleaded insanity but this was dismissed by the judge.

stories.title: Man accused of trying to kill Trump at correspondents' gala agrees to remain jailed
stories.summary: Cole Tomas Allen was also charged on Monday with two additional firearms counts, including discharging a weapon during a crime of violence.

stories.title: Deadly Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon despite ceasefire
stories.summary: At least nine people - including two children - were killed, according to Lebanon's health ministry.

stories.title: Trump says US studying troop cuts in Germany, as spat with Merz intensifies
stories.summary: His remarks come after the German chancellor criticised the US approach to the war in Iran.

stories.title: Israel intercepts Gaza flotilla near Crete and detains 175 activists
stories.summary: Pro-Palestinian activists say 22 boats carrying aid for Gaza were illegally intercepted in international waters near the Greek island.

stories.title: Britney Spears charged in California with driving under influence
stories.summary: The pop star is facing one misdemeanour count of driving under the influence of any alcohol and drug.

stories.title: US singer D4vd bought tools online to dispose of girl's body, prosecutors allege
stories.summary: The Tiktok star's attorneys have maintained his innocence and vowed to "vigorously defend" him.

stories.title: Venice Biennale jury resigns days before start of exhibition
stories.summary: It follows growing tensions over the return of Russia for the first time since the full-scale invasion of Ukraine.

stories.title: China scraps tariffs for all but one African nation
stories.summary: The zero-tariff regime gives China's soft power a boost, but may lead to uneven gains, say analysts.

stories.title: Violence in Australian town after arrest of man over girl's murder
stories.summary: Unrest took place outside hospital where man suspected of murdering a five-year-old girl was being treated.

stories.title: US seeks 'maritime freedom' coalition to restart Strait of Hormuz shipping
stories.summary: Iran effectively closed the crucial waterway when the war broke out on 28 February. Normally one-fifth of the world's oil and liquefied natural gas transits the waterway. Its closure has sent energy prices skyrocketing around the world.

stories.title: 'How are we going to get back home?' Islamist group tightens blockade on Mali capital
stories.summary: The blockade comes days after Mali's defence minister was assassinated in a series of nationwide attacks.

stories.title: Belgium to ban flavored vapes from 2028
stories.summary: Belgium’s federal government on Thursday approved a ban on flavored vapes, with only tobacco-flavored and unflavored e-cigarettes allowed on the market from September 2028. Health Minister Frank Vandenbroucke framed the move as a direct attempt to curb youth addiction. “We want to protect the health of our children and young people and prevent a new generation from becoming dependent on tobacco,” he said in a press statement . “With their attractive flavors, vapes are deliberately designed to lu

stories.title: Kim praises North Korean soldiers who blew themselves up to evade Ukraine capture
stories.summary: Leader mentions for first time lengths to which troops go to avoid falling into enemy hands while fighting for Russia Kim Jong-un has praised North Korean soldiers who blew themselves up with grenades in order to avoid capture while fighting Ukrainian forces in Russia’s western Kursk region, confirming the existence of the extreme battlefield policy. Mounting evidence, including from intelligence reports and testimonies of defectors, has indicated North Korean soldiers are explicitly told to res

stories.title: More private health records of UK Biobank volunteers appear on Chinese website
stories.summary: Patrick Vallance says government working with Chinese officials to remove postings from Alibaba after Biobank data breach last week There have been further listings of confidential health records of UK volunteers on the Chinese website Alibaba since the breach reported last week, and the government is braced for further leaks, the science minister has said. Addressing a House of Lords debate on the attempted sale of data belonging to 500,000 UK Biobank volunteers, Patrick Vallance said the gover

stories.title: South Africa deports Mugabe’s son for unrelated offences after employee shot at family home
stories.summary: Bellarmine Chatunga Mugabe also fined after pleading guilty to immigration and firearms-related offences Two months after an employee was shot in the back at the Mugabe family home in a wealthy suburb of Johannesburg, a South African court has fined and ordered the deportation of Robert Mugabe’s youngest son over two unrelated charges. Bellarmine Chatunga Mugabe, 28, and his cousin Tobias Mugabe Matonhodze, 33, were initially both charged with attempted murder after the incident on 19 February.

stories.title: At least five killed in Israeli strikes on Gaza including a nine-year-old boy
stories.summary: Eighty-one sick and injured children accompanied by their families were evacuated from Gaza to receive medical treatment in Jordan. They travelled via the King Hussein Bridge to various government and private hospitals in the capital Amman.

stories.title: I took an algorithm to court in Sweden. The algorithm won | Charlotta Kronblad
stories.summary: Gothenburg promised to optimise school admissions with a piece of code. The resulting chaos showed how unaccountable systems are ruining lives We like to imagine that injustice announces itself loudly. That when something goes wrong in the public system, alarms go off and someone takes responsibility or is held accountable if they do not. But in 2020 in Gothenburg, injustice arrived quietly, disguised as efficiency. For the first time, the city used an algorithm to allocate places in its schools

stories.title: Jailed Hong Kong pro-democracy activist Jimmy Lai wins free speech award in Germany
stories.summary: Media tycoon honoured in absentia as critics decry his 20-year sentence under national security law The jailed media entrepreneur Jimmy Lai has been awarded Deutsche Welle’s freedom of speech award for his contribution to Hong Kong’s pro-democracy movement. The German public broadcaster said on Thursday that Lai would be presented in absentia with the 12th iteration of the award on 23 June at the DW Global Media Forum in Bonn. Continue reading...

stories.title: Shock in India after man takes remains of his sister to bank to prove her death
stories.summary: Jitu Munda says he was refused access to money in case highlighting ‘lack of humanity’ in Indian bureaucracy The sight of a man bringing the remains of his dead sister to a bank in India after officials had refused to let him withdraw money without proof of her death has caused shock in India. Jitu Munda, 52, from the Indian state of Odisha, was captured on video carrying the remains of his recently deceased sister through the streets of Keonjhar and placing them outside the local bank. Continue

stories.title: Iran supreme leader issues defiant statement on strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: Mojtaba Khamenei says Tehran will eliminate ‘enemy’s abuses of the waterway’ and guard its nuclear and missile programmes US politics live – latest updates Iran’s supreme leader has broken his recent silence with a defiant statement hailing Iran’s control over shipping in the strait of Hormuz and vowing to guard the country’s nuclear and missile programmes. “Today, two months after the largest military deployment and aggression by the world’s bullies in the region, and the United States’ disgrac

stories.title: A Mistral Európa utolsó esélye
stories.summary: 250 éve egy kis angol startup, a Boulton & Watt kulcsszerepet játszott abban, hogy Európa a világ technológiai központjává vált. Most a francia Mistral AI az utolsó esély arra, hogy ne szoruljon végleg a perifériára.

stories.title: Women journalists in Ghana encounter – and overcome – gender barriers
stories.summary: In Ghana, DW Akademie works with women journalists as they try to advance in their careers despite gender barriers.

stories.title: Why UAE's OPEC exit is a blow to Saudi Arabia
stories.summary: The United Arab Emirates is leaving OPEC to pump more oil on its own terms. The break strips Saudi Arabia of a key partner and adds to growing uncertainty over the cartel's future.

stories.title: Israel stops Gaza-bound aid flotilla in international waters
stories.summary: More than 20 ships were intercepted in a mission aimed at challenging the blockade of the Gaza Strip. Israel dismissed those on board as "attention-seeking agitators."

stories.title: India news: Exit polls out in key state elections
stories.summary: Projections suggest mixed outcomes in West Bengal, Kerala, Assam, Puducherry and Tamil Nadu. Narendra Modi's BJP is trying to make inroads in three states where it has never held power.

stories.title: Germany news: Economy shows surprise growth despite Iran war
stories.summary: Germany's economy has grown more than expected, against a backdrop of rising energy costs and uncertain export markets. Meanwhile, VW reports a sharp drop in profits, as pressures mount on the carmaker. DW has more.

stories.title: Hong Kong pro-democracy publisher Jimmy Lai honored by DW
stories.summary: Before being imprisoned for 20 years, Jimmy Lai founded Hong Kong's largest pro-democracy newspaper. His son said the award shows that those who fight for the freedom of others "are never alone."

stories.title: Iran war: Mojtaba Khamenei says there will be 'change' in Strait of Hormuz 'management'
stories.summary: Iran's supreme leader issued a statement implying that Tehran wanted to keep its nuclear program and perhaps impose tolls in the Strait of Hormuz. Crude prices remain volatile. DW has more.

stories.title: Will EU bring in a windfall tax on oil companies?
stories.summary: Five countries, including Germany, want the EU to bring in a so-called windfall tax to target energy companies benefiting from the price crisis. It can be done, but it's complex.

stories.title: First dots on the roadmap to exiting fossil fuels
stories.summary: The message from inaugural talks on exiting fossil fuels was clear. It's not if, but when and how.

stories.title: Belgium seeks nationalization of nuclear power plants
stories.summary: Belgium is reversing its decadeslong phasing-out course, seeking more energy independence by reviving its nuclear plants.

stories.title: How widespread is global fatigue with the US?
stories.summary: The global image of the US has taken a blow, as many people are increasingly fed up with the constant stream of controversies surrounding the Trump administration.

stories.title: Study busts myth: German 'spring fatigue' doesn't exist
stories.summary: Do we really feel more tired in spring? Researchers in Switzerland say, "there's no such thing as spring fatigue." But it is a deep cultural phenomenon in German-speaking countries.

stories.title: Iran's Vafaei playing for more than snooker glory
stories.summary: After years lost to visa battles, Hossein Vafaei is finally delivering on his early promise as a snooker prodigy. He's hoping to inspire people back home amid the turmoil in Iran.

stories.title: UAE oil exit from OPEC signals focus on domestic priorities
stories.summary: Abu Dhabi's exit from OPEC signals a geopolitical shift and a pointed message to Saudi Arabia. What are the ripple effects on ties with Riyadh, the US, and Israel in the short and long term?

stories.title: Trump says he is weighing reducing US troop presence in Germany after Iran feud with Merz
stories.summary: Trump's threat comes after Merz earlier this week said that Washington was being “humiliated” by the Iranian leadership, criticising the US lack of strategy in the war.

stories.title: US lawmakers clash with Hegseth on Iran war and $25 billion price tag
stories.summary: The war has cost $25 billion so far (€21 billion), according to Pentagon figures presented to the House Armed Services Committee.

stories.title: Trash piles up in Bali as landfill ban triggers waste crisis
stories.summary: Bali’s landfill ban leads to waste piling up in streets, burning trash, and health concerns as Indonesia moves to enforce long-delayed rules.

stories.title: Oil temporarily hits $126 wartime high as US–Iran standoff fuels supply fears
stories.summary: Oil markets face upward pressure on Thursday, with Brent crude briefly touching $126 as markets price in tensions in the negotiations between the US and Iran.

stories.title: Nairobi welcomes marathon star Sabastian Sawe after record-breaking London victory
stories.summary: Sabastian Sawe returns to Nairobi after breaking the marathon world record in London, becoming the first runner under two hours in a sanctioned race.

stories.title: Germany’s canal village keeps 129-year mail-by-boat tradition alive
stories.summary: In Germany’s Spreewald, postal worker Andrea Bunar resumes delivering mail by barge, continuing a 129-year tradition in Lehde village.

stories.title: Why is China banning drone sales in Beijing?
stories.summary: The BBC's Laura Bicker looks into why China has tightened regulations on drones, with authorities citing public safety concerns.

stories.title: Trump tightens US naval blockade as Iranian rial collapses to record low
stories.summary: The latest drop follows a currency shock several months ago, when the rial fell from around 1.4 million to 1.6 million per US dollar in under a week, as the war moves turns into an economic clash to exhaust the Iranian regime' options.

stories.title: Thousands join Run for Autism race in Astana to support inclusion
stories.summary: Around 5,000 people took part in the Run for Autism charity race in Astana, supporting inclusion initiatives for children with autism and developmental disabilities.

stories.title: Trainee driver crashes bus into River Seine
stories.summary: The driver hit a parked car and veered off the road into the river - about 12 miles south of Paris - early on Thursday.

stories.title: Egy fideszes kocsmának is jutott 180 millió forint az NKA eltitkolt keretéből
stories.summary: Az R56 egykor Stefka Istvánhoz, a Pesti Srácok munkatársához kötődött.

stories.title: 444: 1,8 milliárdot küldött Mészáros cége a kampányhajrában a DPK-s alapítványnak
stories.summary: A V-Híd három részletben utalt.

stories.title: A választás után fogott össze a Fidesz és a DK a Pest megyében, aztán a DK-s képviselő kilépett a pártjából
stories.summary: A szerdáig a DK-ban politizáló Jószai Teodóra elfogadott a fideszesektől egy jól fizető állást, amit mostantól függetlenként tölt majd be.

stories.title: Százmilliókat fizet valakinek az NKA? Ne bolonduljunk már meg!
stories.summary: Dopeman ötmilliót, fideszes képviselők egyesületei tízmilliókat kaptak az NKA brutális pénzosztásában. Bajnai Zsolt korábbi NKA-s programfelelőssel és Molnár Gábor lemezkiadóval mutatjuk be, miért különösen felháborító az, ahogy Hankó Balázsék elosztogattak több milliárdnyi közpénzt.

stories.title: Óriási mennyiségű adatot loptak el a Mediaworkstől és tettek közzé a dark weben
stories.summary: Összesen 8,5 terabájt adat került a zsarolók birtokába. A 15 millió fájl között bérjegyzékek, szerződések, kimutatások, belső üzenetek is vannak.

stories.title: Sose volt belügyes, még a politikában is újonc, most neki kell visszaterelni a szakmai útra a rendvédelmet
stories.summary: Bár a Tisza Párt operatív vezetője volt, pozíciójából adódóan nem volt a kampány előterében Pósfai Gábor. Mielőtt 2024 végén csatlakozott volna a párthoz, egész felnőtt életében a Decathlonnál dolgozott. Most az lesz a feladata, hogy a rendvédelmet eltávolítsa a politikától.

stories.title: Baán László is lemondott az NKA bizottsági tagságáról
stories.summary: A Szépművészeti Múzeum főigazgatója azután mondott le pozíciójáról, hogy kiderült, az NKA fű alatt osztott ki milliárdokat a Fidesz holdudvarának.

stories.title: Németh Kristóf szerint jó lenne, ha visszajönne a kultúr-tao
stories.summary: A Fidesz-párti színész az RTL-en arról beszélt, hogy az óriásplakátokat a gyereke is szóba hozta az óvodába menet.

stories.summary: Jó hír a csomagolás közben, nem kell törölni a fotókat, SMS-eket.

stories.summary: Tavaly októberben zárták le az ország második legforgalmasabb vasúti mellékvonalát.

stories.summary: Izgalmas lehetett egy 2025 eleji szerkesztőségi megbeszélés a meghekkelt Fidesz-közeli médiabirodalomban.

stories.title: A MOME docense a kuratórium lemondásáról: A legtöbben felszabadult örömmel fogadták
stories.summary: Gyenge Zsolt szerint váratlan volt a kuratórium lemondása.

stories.title: Egy képviselőtársának akart beszólni a dörgicsei polgármester, amikor fogyatékkal élőknek az önkormányzat üléseit ajánlotta programként egy dokumentumban
stories.summary: Pflanzner Sándor saját kezűleg írta bele a szövegbe, hogy az ülések lényegében „Maunika sók”, amik „a trash reality kedvelőnek elégíti ki a lakosság egy csekély szeletének összeesküvés-elméletekkel kapcsolatos igényeit”.

stories.title: Szécsi Zoltán mégsem ül be a parlamentbe
stories.summary: Meggondolta magát, nem csatlakozik a Fidesz-frakcióhoz a háromszoros olimpiai bajnok vízilabdázó.

stories.title: Student’s alleged jailing in China over Australian pro-democracy protests sparks calls for inquiry
stories.summary: Human rights commissioner says alleged jailing highlights the ‘growing risks of transnational repression’ in Australia Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Australia’s human rights commissioner has said the Chinese student who was allegedly jailed for six years by Chinese authorities for joining protests in Sydney underscores the “very real and growing risks of transnational repression affecting people in Australia –

stories.title: UK: London stabbing being treated as 'terrorist incident'
stories.summary: UK police have declared the stabbing of two men in a traditionally Jewish area of London as a "terrorist incident." The victims are in a stable condition. Prime Minister Starmer called the incident "deeply concerning."

stories.title: Israeli strikes kill eight in southern Lebanon, including three rescue workers
stories.summary: Israel has been fighting Hezbollah since early March, sending troops into south Lebanon to battle the Iran-backed militant group, with the violence ongoing despite a shaky ongoing ceasefire.

stories.title: US blockade of Iranian ports aims at 'internal division,' parliament speaker says
stories.summary: Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, a powerful figure, has grown in prominence since the start of the war and was the lead negotiator in the so far only round of direct US-Iranian talks.

stories.title: US has 'no closer friends than the British,' Trump tells King Charles III at White House ceremony
stories.summary: Trump’s up-and-down relationship with UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer has taken a particularly sour turn over the past several months as the president has sought to rally international support for the Iran war.

stories.title: Births, deaths and a first kiss: life near the frontline in Ukraine – in pictures
stories.summary: Aria Shahrokhshahi went to teenage discos and hospital wards rattled by rockets in order to capture how it feels when your country is dragged into a war Continue reading...

stories.title: Family of ailing Iranian Nobel laureate say keeping her in jail is a death sentence
stories.summary: Narges Mohammadi denied medical leave from prison in spite of sharp decline in health and drastic weight loss, say lawyers The family of the jailed Iranian Nobel laureate Narges Mohammadi say they fear for her life after a sharp deterioration in her health, suspected heart attack and drop in body weight of almost 20kg (44lb). The 54-year-old human rights activist, who was awarded the 2023 Nobel peace prize while in prison , had been released for health reasons in 2024. She was re-arrested in Dec

stories.title: Christchurch gunman fails in bid to appeal against guilty pleas in New Zealand court
stories.summary: Australian white supremacist who murdered 51 Muslims said poor mental health made him admit to crimes The Australian white supremacist who murdered 51 Muslim worshippers at two mosques in Christchurch in 2019 has been prevented from appealing against his guilty pleas, after one of New Zealand’s highest courts said his bid was “utterly devoid of merit”. Brenton Tarrant, who is responsible for the worst mass shooting in New Zealand’s history, asked the court of appeal in February to allow him to a

stories.title: Nem lehet többé drónokat venni Pekingben, a használatukat is szigorú szabályokhoz kötik
stories.summary: Peking teljes légterét már tavaly drónmentes övezetté nyilvánították.

stories.summary: A korrupcióellenes szervezet szerint kartellgyanú is felmerül érintett cégeknél.

stories.title: Korszakváltás a parlamentben: évtizedek óta bebetonozott fideszesek is távoznak
stories.summary: A Fidesz–KDNP 99 képviselője veszíti el a mandátumát májusban, közülük öten már 1990-ben bent ültek a parlamentben. Egyedül Németh Zsolt marad közülük képviselő, a tizedik ciklusával ő lesz a legtapasztaltabb tagja a következő Országgyűlésnek. Megnézheti, hány évet húztak le a parlamentben a most kieső és a parlamenti munkát folytató képviselők.

stories.title: Nem számol visszamenőleg magasabb babavárós büntetőkamatot az MBH, adminisztratív hibára hivatkoznak
stories.summary: Az MBH közlése szerint haladéktalanul megkezdték a szóban forgó ügyek elszámolásának helyesbítését, az érintetteket pedig megkeresik, hogy senkit se érjen hátrány.

stories.title: Négyéves rekordra kergette az olajárat Trump harciassága
stories.summary: Reggel 126 dollár fölé emelkedett az olaj világpiaci ára, miután egyre kilátástalanabb az iráni háború gyors rendezése. Bár azóta volt némi korrekció, nyárra akár 190 dolláros ár is elképzelhető.

stories.title: Kilépett a DK-ból, mozgalmat alapít Kiss László óbudai polgármester
stories.summary: Szerinte a pártok és a frakciók kora lejárt.

stories.summary: Gulyás Gergely közzétette, kiket ültetne a Fidesz a szakbizottságokba.

stories.summary: A legtöbb közpénz, összesen 308 milliárd forint a Tiborcz István tulajdonába tartozó Gránit Alapkezelő által kezelt 6 tőkealapnál landolt.

stories.title: Az Európai Bizottság vizsgálatot indított legalább egy, a Mollal kötött megállapodása miatt
stories.summary: Ugyanabból lehet gond, mint ami miatt a modellváltó egyetemekről nem lehet Erasmusra menni.

stories.title: Lemondott a MOME teljes kuratóriuma
stories.summary: Egy levélben azt írja Böszörményi-Nagy Gergely kuratóriumi elnök, hogy felajánlják az egyetemet fenntartó alapítvány alapítói jogait a felsőoktatásért felelős, újonnan felálló minisztériumnak. Az egyetemet hat éve szervezték ki alapítványi fenntartásba.

stories.title: 2025 az éghajlatváltozás ijesztő éve volt, az északi sarkkör közelében is mértek 30 fokot
stories.summary: Európa a leggyorsabban melegedő kontinens, 95 százalékán melegebb volt, mint a globális átlaghőmérséklet. A hó és jég is ijesztő ütemben tűnik el.

stories.title: Az SZFE vezetése méltatlan magánperformansznak nevezte, hogy a hallgatói fórumon lemondásra szólították fel a rektort
stories.summary: Többek között azt írták, a színpadon helyet foglaló egyetemi vezetők higgadtan fogadták a véleménynyilvánítás nem szokványos formáját.

stories.title: 10,5 milliárdos osztalékot fizet az Opus Global, több mint 5 milliárd Mészáros Lőrincé lesz
stories.summary: A cég közgyűlése után Mészáros közölte, hogy a részére megállapított osztalék összegét meghaladó mértékben akar saját részvényeket venni.

stories.title: Olajos eső és toronymagas lángok: hatalmas tüzeket okoznak az ukrán dróntámadások Oroszországban
stories.summary: Tuapsze kikötővárosa után újabb olajipari létesítményeket támadnak az ukránok, ami komoly ökológiai veszélyt is jelent.

stories.title: Felmászott a Mol-torony tetejére, és ejtőernyővel kiugrott egy külföldi férfi
stories.summary: Senkinek nem esett baja, de a rendőrség eljárást indított. Videó készült a hajmeresztő akcióról.

stories.title: Házkutatást tartottak Sára Botond lakásán a 70 ezer forintos bor ügyében
stories.summary: A főispán szerint az ügy „bagatell, nevetséges, koncepciós”.

stories.title: „Nagyon mérges vagyok Matolcsy úrra” – a kecskeméti egyetemi alapítvány új elnöke hasznos idiótákról és több mint 100 milliárd forintnyi pénz kilapátolásáról beszélt
stories.summary: Nyilvánosság elé állt az MNB-s játszmába 127 milliárd forinttal belecsúszó kecskeméti egyetemi alapítvány új vezetője, aki egy év után előadta a történtekről a saját verziójukat. Boross Ildikó szerint náluk „ártatlanok és hasznos idióták” ültek, a vagyont pedig az MNB és az Optima régi vezetése vesztegette el.

stories.title: Germany news: Cabinet discusses 2027 budget plans as Iran war threatens finances
stories.summary: The Cabinet hopes to sign off on core points of its 2027 budget, so it can start to move through parliament. Germany already plans massive borrowing next year, before adequately factoring in Iran-based costs. Follow DW.

stories.title: 'Rose' and the piece of fabric that embodies gender politics
stories.summary: As the film starring Sandra Hüller is released in Germany, DW talked to director Markus Schleinzer about why women passing as men inspired his historical drama, and why it's a current topic.

stories.title: Why Germany wants a seat at the UN Security Council
stories.summary: With the rules-based world order "under stress," Germany is on a mission to gain a coveted seat at the United Nations' top table. German Foreign Minister Johann Wadephul told DW why diplomacy still matters.

stories.title: 2026 Goethe Medal: Meet the recipients
stories.summary: Though they work in different fields and countries, what unites them is their focus on a Europe in transition: Arvo Pärt, Anita Raja and Prodromos Tsinikoris have been awarded the 2026 Goethe Medal.

stories.title: Germany: Merz hails 'historic' health care reform
stories.summary: The Cabinet has greenlit a health care draft law designed to lower insurance rates. The bill heads to parliament next, but doctors' associations and patient protection advocates say it amounts to austerity measures.

stories.title: Russia to hold Victory Day parade without weaponry display
stories.summary: The scaled back parade comes amid increasing Ukrainian attacks deep into mainland Russia, including the capital Moscow. The last time a Victory Day parade was held without weaponry display was in 2007.

stories.title: Teenage bullying increases in Ukrainian schools
stories.summary: The number of teenage victims of bullying is rising in Ukrainian schools. Experts attribute this to war trauma and chronic stress among teenagers.

stories.title: What are the sticking points for the EU's new budget?
stories.summary: The battle over the next long-term EU budget has begun. The EU's institutions have staked out their positions and are seeking significantly more funding, but it is the 27 member states that will ultimately decide.

stories.title: King Charles commemorates 9/11 victims in New York visit
stories.summary: The royal couple laid a bouquet at the September 11 Memorial and met with victim's families.

stories.title: Trump threatens US troop cuts in Germany
stories.summary: Donald Trump says the US is considering a "possible reduction" of its forces in Germany just days after German Chancellor Friedrich Merz said the US was being "humiliated" by Iran's leadership.

stories.title: Whale 'Timmy' reaches Denmark in rescue effort — in pictures
stories.summary: A barge carrying a humpback whale stranded in Germany for weeks has reached Danish waters on its journey to the North Sea.

stories.title: Watch: Key moments from King Charles III’s address to US Congress
stories.summary: King Charles III addressed US Congress, stressing the importance of the UK-US partnership in facing global challenges. Lawmakers applauded as he mixed humor, history and forward-looking remarks in a rare speech by a British monarch.

stories.title: Greenpeace stages beach protest against fossil fuels during Colombia conference
stories.summary: Greenpeace activists displayed large messages on a beach in Colombia, calling for a transition away from fossil fuels during an international conference.

stories.title: Elderly woman rescued by autonomous robot near Ukraine frontline
stories.summary: Video released by Ukraine’s Third Army Corps shows a four-hour rescue in Lyman, where a 77-year-old woman was saved by an autonomous vehicle after being spotted by drone pilots. The ground drone carried a blanket and a note reading: “Grandma, sit down!”

stories.title: Hundreds gather to smell rare ‘corpse flower’ blooming in London
stories.summary: Visitors flocked to Kew Gardens in London to witness and smell the rare blooming of the titan arum, known for its foul odour.

stories.title: US opioid crisis: families speak out after Purdue Pharma sentencing
stories.summary: Relatives of opioid overdose victims reacted outside a US court after Purdue Pharma was sentenced over its role in the opioid crisis.

stories.title: Iran executed 21 people and arrested thousands since start of war, UN warns
stories.summary: UN has warned that the Tehran regime has executed 21 people and detained over 4,000 since the war began, accusing the Islamic Republic of torture, disappearances and targeting minorities.

stories.title: Gulf unity on display in Jeddah, but absences and signals reveal limits to coordination
stories.summary: Gulf leaders met in Saudi Arabia to address the fallout from the Iran war, projecting unity but stopping short of concrete steps as divisions and uncertainty persist.

stories.title: In five charts - How UAE's exit could affect Opec's influence over the oil price
stories.summary: The BBC takes a look in charts at what the UAE's departure could mean for the oil cartel and more widely.

stories.title: Video shows suspect approaching police after stabbing in north London
stories.summary: Images shared on social media show police tasering and arresting a man with a knife in Golders Green in north London, after two Jewish men were stabbed in an attack. Police declared it a "terrorist incident" and said a 45-year-old man had been arrested, adding that he had also tried to stab officers

stories.title: Three takeaways from Hegseth's clash with lawmakers over Iran war
stories.summary: The hours-long hearing was the first time the US defence secretary had faced questions under oath since the conflict began.

stories.title: A son overlooked and a jailed tycoon: Inside Samsung's succession drama
stories.summary: The family dynasty behind Samsung is so complicated it regularly makes headline news in South Korea.

stories.title: The sub-Arctic town pitching itself as Canada's gateway to Europe
stories.summary: Churchill, Manitoba, best known as the world's polar bear capital, has ambitions of making its deep-water port a year-round trade route.

stories.title: Indians lost $2.5bn to digital fraud in 2025 - now its central bank is fighting back
stories.summary: The RBI is mulling measures to tackle rising cases of digital fraud in India - but will they work?

stories.title: The King and Queen in the Big Apple: What the royals did on their third day in the US
stories.summary: After visiting the 9/11 Memorial, Queen Camilla read to children at the New York Public Library and King Charles visited a community organisation in Harlem.

stories.title: Watch: Aerial video shows destruction after tornado strikes small Texas town
stories.summary: A severe storm outbreak has been battering parts of the Midwest and Southern United States for nearly a week.

stories.title: 'Attacked 28 times in a day' - BBC visits heavily targeted US-UK base in Iraq
stories.summary: Before the fragile ceasefire in the region, this was one of the most heavily targeted US and UK military bases in the Middle East.

stories.title: Joy as record-breaking runner Sawe returns home
stories.summary: BBC reports from Kenya, home to Sabastian Sawe, the first man to run a marathon in under two hours.

stories.title: Sri Lanka police arrest 22 Buddhist monks after 110kg of cannabis found in luggage
stories.summary: Customs officials say group allegedly hid 5kg of ‘kush’ in false walls of bags on return from Bangkok holiday Twenty-two Buddhist monks are in Sri Lankan police custody after customs officials found 110kg of high-grade cannabis concealed in their luggage, the largest ever drug bust at Colombo’s main international airport. The group, mostly junior monks in training from temples across Sri Lanka, were alleged to have “carried about five kilos of the narcotic concealed within false walls in their l

stories.title: Russia claims its Africa Corps group prevented coup in Mali after rebels seize towns
stories.summary: Kremlin-controlled paramilitaries also alleged it inflicted ‘irreplaceable losses’ on insurgents avoiding civilian casualties Russia’s defence ministry has claimed its Africa Corps – the successor to the former Wagner mercenary group – prevented a coup in Mali over the weekend, avoiding mass civilian casualties and inflicting “irreplaceable losses” on rebel insurgents. It said in a statement that its troops in the desert town of Kidal near the Algerian border had fought for more than 24 hours wh

stories.title: Trumps greet King Charles III and Queen Camilla at the White House
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump and First Lady Melania Trump greet Britain's King Charles III and Queen Camilla upon arrival at the South Portico of the White House in Washington, DC.

stories.title: Dozens of arrests made in Turkey ahead of 1 May celebrations, Istanbul prosecutor says
stories.summary: The day, which celebrates workers and the working classes, usually sees a major police deployment in Turkey every year.

stories.title: Ex-FBI Director Comey indicted in probe over online post officials say constituted Trump threat
stories.summary: Trump fired Comey in May 2017 amid an FBI investigation into potential ties between Russia and Trump’s presidential campaign.

stories.title: Fears of resurgence in Somali piracy after three vessels hijacked in a week
stories.summary: Pirates appear to be taking advantage of international naval strength being diverted to Middle East Three vessels have been hijacked off the coast of Somalia in the past week, raising fears of a resurgence in piracy around the Horn of Africa, and adding to the woes of the global shipping industry. The merchant vessel Sward was taken over on 26 April, a day after a dhow was seized. These followed the 21 April hijacking of Honour 25, a motor tanker carrying 18,000 barrels of oil, according to the

stories.title: Germany news: Major raids carried out against Hells Angels
stories.summary: A large-scale police operation in the western state of North Rhine-Westphalia has targeted biker gang crime. Meanwhile, Germany is to bid again for a non-permanent seat in the UN Security Council.

stories.title: 'Marine unicorns' aren't loving Arctic noise
stories.summary: Narwhals are fleeing Canada's far north. Researchers suspect a link to noise pollution from increasing ship traffic.

stories.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy rebukes Israel for buying grain 'stolen' by Russia
stories.summary: President Volodymyr Zelenskyy has accused Israel of "purchasing stolen goods," namely grain from regions occupied by Russia. Ukraine summoned Israel's ambassador on Monday, and now it says another such ship has docked.

stories.title: What Renault's new EV reveals about the global auto industry
stories.summary: French carmaker Renault no longer sells its brand in the Chinese market, but it's not afraid to design and develop them there. It's just one more sign of big shifts in the global auto industry.

stories.title: Germany's entrepreneurs betting on post-Maduro Venezuela
stories.summary: Venezuela's economy is opening sectors to foreign firms, and that could pay off for the few German companies that have stuck it out through years of decline.

stories.title: How a controversial gas pipeline deal could further stall Bosnia's EU accession
stories.summary: A US backed gas pipeline could help Bosnia cut reliance on Russian energy. But Brussels warns that a deal with a company linked to Trump allies could put the country's EU hopes at risk.

stories.title: 'Only yes means yes' rape definition backed by EU lawmakers
stories.summary: The European Parliament voted for a consent-based definition of rape. But what constitutes rape under criminal law still varies significantly across the bloc.

stories.title: Extreme weather and green energy on the rise in Europe
stories.summary: Europe's climate extremes have hit new highs — but renewables are now supplying nearly half of the continent's electricity.

stories.title: Rare spring snowstorm hits Moscow
stories.summary: A rare spring snowfall blankets Moscow, forcing flight cancellations and toppling trees. The Russian Meteorological Center has declared an orange alert level for the capital, in effect until Tuesday morning.

stories.title: Iran to reopen Strait of Hormuz if US lifts blockade, reports claim
stories.summary: Despite an ongoing ceasefire, the US and Iran are locked in a standoff over the Strait of Hormuz, through which a fifth of the world’s oil and gas trade passes in peacetime.

stories.title: Iran parades armed women in streets as questions over AI videos grow
stories.summary: Videos emerging on social media from Iran appear to show armed Iranian women parading with IRGC in several cities in an upsurge of nationalist and religious fervour, dubbed as a “jihad of women”, but some clips spark AI editing doubts and remain unverified.
